title: Linting WIDTHEXPAND, WIDTHTRUNC for if branches inside always on parameter as condition
body:
Run into a bit of a weird code, but I still think its valid.
However verilator is throwing linting warning, so I just want to leave it here.

```
module test #(
    parameter SECDED = 2
)(
    output logic [11+SECDED:0] code_o
);

    always_comb begin
        if (SECDED == 0) begin
            code_o = 12'h1;
        end else
            code_o = {SECDED+12{1'b0}};
    end

endmodule
```

The first branch if(SECDED==0) is excluded with a constant parameter SECDED, so the code inside the block should be irrelevant.
It still reports a WIDTHEXPAND lint warning.
This code can be fixed with if outside always and assign statements, however, I think they are equivalent and both should not be linting warnings?
Probably the problem is with verilator not checking if branch condition is a parameter or a constant if its inside a always block.

Error:
```
%Warning-WIDTHEXPAND: test.v:9:20: Operator ASSIGN expects 14 bits on the Assign RHS, but Assign RHS's CONST '12'h1' generates 12 bits.
                                 : ... In instance test
    9 |             code_o = 12'h1;
      |                    ^
                      ... For warning description see https://verilator.org/warn/WIDTHEXPAND?v=5.012
                      ... Use "/* verilator lint_off WIDTHEXPAND */" and lint_on around source to disable this message.
%Error: Exiting due to 1 warning(s
```

verilator --version

```
Verilator 5.012 2023-06-13 rev v5.012 (mod)
```


May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?

Sure, not sure when I will have time though. Just reporting it for later.
